subject: Statistics
title: STL Decomposition
description: A micro-lesson that makes STL Decomposition usable.

hook.text: Classical decomposition breaks if the seasonal pattern changes. Modern methods handle evolving seasons.

buildup.text: STL — Seasonal and Trend decomposition using Loess — uses local regression to extract flexible components.

discovery.text: Unlike classical methods, STL allows the seasonal component to change gradually over time.

twist.text: Christmas shopping season has grown longer over decades. STL captures this shift; classical decomposition can't.

climax.text: STL is robust to outliers and handles missing data gracefully. It's become the default decomposition method.

punchline.text: Seasons change. Your decomposition method should too. STL adapts.

quiz.question: What advantage does STL decomposition have over classical methods?
options: It allows the seasonal component to change gradually over time
It only decomposes trends, not seasonality
